title: Specialization and promotion in an academic discipline
abstract: What does it take to get tenure in an academic discipline? The receipt of tenure has received less attention in the research literature on scientific careers than other career outcomes. To explain variation in the risk of receiving tenure, we theorize that the extent of specialization in scholars' research programs should improve promotion prospects, especially for men. Using data on sociology PhD recipients in 1972-1976, we construct a measure of research specialization and add it to a traditional attainment model that includes productivity, visibility, and prestige of specialty area(s). We find that a high degree of specialization actually decreases promotion prospects, at least for men. (C) 2009 International Sociological Association Research Committee 28 on Social Stratification and Mobility. Published by Elsevier Ltd. All rights reserved.
authors_text: Leahey, Erin; Keith, Bruce; Crockett, Jason
journal: Research in Social Stratification and Mobility
